ddls:
CREATE TABLE "complaints" (
  "complaint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each complaint */,
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Borough where the complaint was reported */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Type of noise complaint */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Type of location where the noise occurred */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* User who reported the complaint */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the noise complaint */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the noise complaint */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the complaint was reported */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the complaint */,
  "assigned_to" INTEGER /* Employee assigned to handle the complaint */,
  "resolution_details" TEXT /* Details of the actions taken to resolve the complaint */,
  "feedback" TEXT /* Feedback provided by the resident after the complaint is resolved */,
  "channel" TEXT /* Channel through which the complaint was reported */,
  "urgency" TEXT /* Urgency level of the complaint */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("complaint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_complaints_borough_id FOREIGN KEY ("borough_id") REFERENCES boroughs ("borough_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_complaints_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES complaint_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_complaints_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES location_types ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_complaints_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_complaints_assigned_to FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_to") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "boroughs" (
  "borough_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each borough */,
  "borough_name" TEXT /* Name of the borough */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the borough's authorities */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the borough */,
  "area" REAL /* Area of the borough in square kilometers */,
  "noise_hotspots" TEXT /* List of known noise hotspots in the borough */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("borough_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "complaint_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each type of complaint */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the complaint type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the complaint type */,
  "severity_level" INTEGER /* Severity level of the complaint type */,
  "response_time" INTEGER /* Expected response time in hours for the complaint type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_types" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location type */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the location type */,
  "noise_limit" REAL /* Maximum allowed noise level for the location type in decibels */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Residential address of the user */,
  "registered_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user registered */,
  "notification_preference" TEXT /* User's preference for receiving notifications */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "employee_name" TEXT /* Name of the employee */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the employee */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the employee belongs to */,
  "position" TEXT /* Position of the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actions" (
  "action_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each action */,
  "complaint_id" INTEGER /* Complaint to which the action is linked */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Employee who took the action */,
  "action_description" TEXT /* Description of the action taken */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the action was taken */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("action_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actions_complaint_id FOREIGN KEY ("complaint_id") REFERENCES complaints ("complaint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_actions_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
